Talks

Talks

Systems, observability, and security. Research talks, seminars, guest lectures, and practitioner presentations.

Speaking themes

Topics I can present now

From active projects, published work, and recurring research themes.

Reducing eBPF tracing overhead

Measurement overhead costs, untraced process impact, instrumentation design that preserves usefulness without distorting workloads.

Linux performance investigation

Reasoning about system bottlenecks, designing performance experiments, evidence-based optimization.

Security in operational and embedded contexts

Platform security questions spanning Android, IoT, industrial environments, and the systems constraints that make visibility difficult.

From research idea to working artifact

Ideas into software, measurements, documentation, and workflows usable beyond prototypes.

Recent

Results and milestones

10 February, 2026
Submitted manuscript: ZeroDown, a zero-downtime IoT policy enforcement framework for MCU-class devices
18 October, 2025
Manuscript update: BlueSentry, a runtime eBPF policy framework for BLE security on embedded devices
04 August, 2024
Paper published: Eliminating eBPF Tracing Overhead on Untraced Processes
15 November, 2022
Paper published: HELOT: Hunting Evil Life in Operational Technology

Formats

Presentation formats

  • Research seminars for systems, security, networking, and performance audiences.
  • Guest lectures on Linux internals, eBPF, benchmarking, and engineering methodology.
  • Practitioner talks that connect low-level systems ideas to practical engineering decisions.
  • Paper walkthroughs that emphasize experiment design, limits, and reproducibility.

Invite

Invitations

Talks work best when scoped to a concrete systems question. Reach out with your audience and problem focus.

Invite Publications Research
